questions.questionText: The nerve most commonly injured in fracture of shaft of humerus is:
questions.explanation: Radial nerve lies in spiral groove of humerus.

questions.questionText: Erb's palsy involves injury to nerve roots:
questions.explanation: Erb-Duchenne palsy results from upper trunk (C5-C6) injury.

questions.questionText: Klumpke's paralysis is due to injury to:
questions.explanation: Klumpke's palsy involves lower trunk (C8-T1).

questions.questionText: Anatomical snuffbox contains which artery?
questions.explanation: Radial artery crosses the floor of anatomical snuffbox.

questions.questionText: Claw hand deformity is caused by injury to:
questions.explanation: Ulnar nerve injury causes claw hand.

questions.questionText: Nerve passing through carpal tunnel:
questions.explanation: Median nerve passes through carpal tunnel.

questions.questionText: Winging of scapula is due to paralysis of:
questions.explanation: Long thoracic nerve supplies serratus anterior.

questions.questionText: Saturday night palsy involves which nerve?
questions.explanation: Compression of radial nerve causes wrist drop.

questions.questionText: Ape thumb deformity is caused by injury to:
questions.explanation: Median nerve injury causes loss of thenar apex.

questions.questionText: The rotator cuff muscle NOT attached to greater tubercle:
questions.explanation: Subscapularis inserts on lesser tubercle.

questions.questionText: Axillary nerve injury occurs in fracture of:
questions.explanation: Axillary nerve winds around surgical neck.

questions.questionText: Cubitus valgus with ulnar nerve palsy is seen in:
questions.explanation: Non-union of lateral condyle leads to tardy ulnar nerve palsy.

questions.questionText: Hand of benediction is seen in injury to:
questions.explanation: High median nerve lesion causes hand of benediction.

questions.questionText: Froment's sign is positive in lesion of:
questions.explanation: Froment's sign tests adductor pollicis.

questions.questionText: The dermatome of the thumb is:
questions.explanation: C6 dermatome covers the thumb.

questions.questionText: Nerve injured in fracture neck of fibula:
questions.explanation: Common peroneal nerve winds around neck of fibula.

questions.questionText: Trendelenburg test is positive when which muscle is paralyzed?
questions.explanation: Gluteus medius is the chief hip abductor.

questions.questionText: Foot drop is caused by injury to:
questions.explanation: Common peroneal nerve supplies ankle dorsiflexors.

questions.questionText: Femoral nerve injury causes loss of:
questions.explanation: Femoral nerve supplies quadriceps femoris.

questions.questionText: Great saphenous vein drains into:
questions.explanation: Great saphenous vein drains into femoral vein.

questions.questionText: The nerve of the medial compartment of thigh:
questions.explanation: Obturator nerve (L2-L4) supplies adductor group.

questions.questionText: Injury to superior gluteal nerve affects:
questions.explanation: Superior gluteal nerve supplies gluteus medius/minimus.

questions.questionText: The structure passing through adductor canal:
questions.explanation: Femoral artery passes through adductor canal.

questions.questionText: Locking of knee joint is caused by injury to:
questions.explanation: Torn meniscus fragment can cause mechanical locking.

questions.questionText: Anterior drawer test is positive in injury to:
questions.explanation: Anterior drawer test assesses ACL integrity.

questions.questionText: The muscle that unlocks the knee joint:
questions.explanation: Popliteus laterally rotates femur to unlock knee.

questions.questionText: The artery at risk in supracondylar fracture of femur:
questions.explanation: Popliteal artery is closely related to posterior distal femur.

questions.questionText: Dorsalis pedis artery is a continuation of:
questions.explanation: Anterior tibial artery continues as dorsalis pedis.

questions.questionText: Nerve supply of adductor magnus:
questions.explanation: Adductor magnus has dual nerve supply.

questions.questionText: The ligament preventing posterior dislocation of tibia:
questions.explanation: PCL prevents posterior displacement of tibia.

questions.questionText: Sternal angle (angle of Louis) is at level of:
questions.explanation: Sternal angle marks T4-T5 disc level.

questions.questionText: Right recurrent laryngeal nerve hooks around:
questions.explanation: Right RLN hooks around right subclavian artery.

questions.questionText: Oblique fissure of lung corresponds to:
questions.explanation: Oblique fissure runs from T3 spine to 6th costochondral junction.

questions.questionText: The structure crossing arch of aorta:
questions.explanation: Left phrenic nerve crosses arch anteriorly.

questions.questionText: Thoracic duct drains into:
questions.explanation: Thoracic duct drains into left venous angle.

questions.questionText: Bronchopulmonary segment supplied by:
questions.explanation: Segment is supplied by a tertiary bronchus.

questions.questionText: Commonest site of foreign body impaction in esophagus:
questions.explanation: Cricopharyngeus is the narrowest site.

questions.questionText: Surface marking of apex of heart:
questions.explanation: Apex beat is at left 5th ICS, midclavicular line.

questions.questionText: Right coronary artery supplies:
questions.explanation: RCA supplies SA node in 60% of cases.

questions.questionText: Coarctation of aorta most commonly occurs:
questions.explanation: Coarctation typically occurs distal to left subclavian.

questions.questionText: Azygos vein drains into:
questions.explanation: Azygos vein drains into the SVC.

questions.questionText: All are posterior mediastinum contents EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Heart is in the middle mediastinum.

questions.questionText: Needle thoracocentesis for tension pneumothorax done at:
questions.explanation: Emergency decompression at 2nd ICS, midclavicular line.

questions.questionText: First rib has groove for:
questions.explanation: First rib has grooves for subclavian vessels.

questions.questionText: Diaphragm main nerve supply:
questions.explanation: Phrenic nerve provides motor and sensory supply.

questions.questionText: McBurney's point is at:
questions.optionB: Junction of med 1/3 and lat 2/3 of inguinal ligament
questions.optionC: Junction of lat 1/3 and med 2/3 of ASIS-umbilicus line
questions.explanation: McBurney's point is surface marking for appendix base.

questions.questionText: Hesselbach's triangle bounded by all EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Inguinal ligament is the inferior boundary.

questions.questionText: Direct inguinal hernia occurs:
questions.explanation: Direct hernia passes medial to inferior epigastric vessels.

questions.questionText: Portal vein formed by union of:
questions.explanation: Portal vein forms behind neck of pancreas.

questions.questionText: Calot's triangle contains:
questions.explanation: Calot's triangle contains cystic artery.

questions.questionText: Transpyloric plane of Addison is level of:
questions.explanation: Transpyloric plane is at L1 level.

questions.questionText: Porto-systemic anastomosis at lower esophagus involves:
questions.explanation: This causes esophageal varices.

questions.questionText: Meckel's diverticulum is remnant of:
questions.explanation: Meckel's is a remnant of the vitello-intestinal duct.

questions.questionText: Narrowest part of male urethra:
questions.explanation: External urethral meatus is the narrowest part.

questions.questionText: Pouch of Douglas is:
questions.explanation: Rectouterine pouch is the most dependent peritoneal recess.

questions.questionText: Blood supply of stomach along greater curvature:
questions.explanation: Supplied by right and left gastroepiploic arteries.

questions.questionText: Structures through aortic hiatus (T12):
questions.explanation: Aortic hiatus transmits aorta, thoracic duct, azygos vein.

questions.questionText: Kidney related posteriorly to all EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Liver is related anteriorly to right kidney.

questions.questionText: Spleen related to ribs EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Spleen is related to 9th, 10th, 11th ribs.

questions.questionText: Epiploic foramen anterior boundary:
questions.explanation: Anterior boundary contains portal triad.

questions.questionText: Middle meningeal artery enters skull through:
questions.explanation: Enters via foramen spinosum.

questions.questionText: Facial nerve exits skull through:
questions.explanation: Exits via stylomastoid foramen.

questions.questionText: Bell's palsy is lesion of:
questions.explanation: Bell's palsy is idiopathic LMN facial palsy.

questions.questionText: Parotid duct opens opposite:
questions.explanation: Opens into vestibule opposite upper 2nd molar.

questions.questionText: Dangerous area of face drains into:
questions.explanation: Drains to cavernous sinus via ophthalmic veins.

questions.questionText: Tongue muscles supplied by hypoglossal EXCET:
questions.explanation: Palatoglossus is supplied by vagus.

questions.questionText: Trotter's triad seen in:
questions.explanation: Triad: deafness, palatal palsy, trigeminal neuralgia.

questions.questionText: Taste from anterior 2/3 of tongue via:
questions.explanation: Chorda tympani carries taste.

questions.questionText: Frey syndrome is complication of surgery on:
questions.explanation: Frey syndrome is gustatory sweating.

questions.questionText: External carotid artery branches:
questions.explanation: ECA has 8 branches.

questions.questionText: Muscle opening the jaw:
questions.explanation: Lateral pterygoid depresses mandible.

questions.questionText: Cavernous sinus contains EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: V2 is in the lateral wall, not sinus itself.

questions.questionText: Killian's dehiscence located in:
questions.explanation: Gap in inferior constrictor.

questions.questionText: Thyroid gland develops from:
questions.explanation: Develops from foramen cecum via thyroglossal duct.

questions.questionText: Nerve supply of platysma:
questions.explanation: Supplied by cervical branch of facial nerve.

questions.questionText: Cerebrospinal fluid is produced primarily by:
questions.explanation: Choroid plexus in the ventricles produces CSF.

questions.questionText: Broca's area is located in the:
questions.explanation: Broca's motor speech area is in the inferior frontal gyrus of the dominant hemisphere.

questions.questionText: The primary visual cortex is located in the:
questions.explanation: Area 17 is located in the occipital lobe around the calcarine fissure.

questions.questionText: Wernicke's aphasia results from damage to the:
questions.explanation: Wernicke's sensory speech area is in the posterior part of the superior temporal gyrus.

questions.questionText: The decussation of the pyramids occurs in the:
questions.explanation: Motor fibers cross at the pyramidal decussation in the lower medulla.

questions.questionText: Which cranial nerve emerges from the dorsal aspect of the brainstem?
questions.explanation: Only the trochlear nerve emerges from the dorsal surface of the midbrain.

questions.questionText: The major relay station for sensory information to the cerebral cortex is:
questions.explanation: The thalamus relays all sensory modalities except olfaction to the cortex.

questions.questionText: Parkinson's disease is associated with degeneration of neurons in the:
questions.explanation: Dopaminergic depletion from pars compacta of substantia nigra causes Parkinson's.

questions.questionText: The artery usually involved in an epidural hematoma is the:
questions.explanation: Rupture of MMA at the pterion causes epidural hematoma.

questions.questionText: The representation of the lower limb in the motor homunculus is supplied by:
questions.explanation: ACA supplies the medial surface where lower limb represents.

questions.questionText: Fertilization usually occurs in the:
questions.explanation: Ampulla is the widest part where fertilization typically occurs.

questions.questionText: The notochord induces the formation of the:
questions.explanation: Notochord signals the overlying ectoderm to form the neural plate.

questions.questionText: The first germ layer to form during gastrulation is:
questions.explanation: Epiblast cells displace hypoblast to form definitive endoderm first.

questions.questionText: Meckel's cartilage is a derivative of which pharyngeal arch?
questions.explanation: First arch cartilage forms malleus, incus, and sphenomandibular ligament.

questions.questionText: The thyroid gland develops mainly from the:
questions.explanation: An endodermal downgrowth from the foramen cecum forms the thyroid.

questions.questionText: Tetralogy of Fallot is a defect in the development of the:
questions.explanation: Anterior displacement of the conotruncal septum causes TOF.

questions.questionText: The paramesonephric (Mullerian) ducts in females develop into all EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Lower 1/3 of vagina develops from the urogenital sinus.

questions.questionText: Cleft lip is caused by failure of fusion of:
questions.explanation: Failure of fusion causes anterior cleft anomalies.

questions.questionText: The kidney (metanephros) begins to produce urine by:
questions.explanation: Urine production starts around weeks 10-12 to contribute to amniotic fluid.

questions.questionText: Which structure is a derivative of the midgut?
questions.explanation: Appendix, cecum, ascending colon are midgut derivatives.

questions.questionText: Transitional epithelium (urothelium) is characteristically found in the:
questions.explanation: Urothelium lines the urinary tract from renal calyces to proximal urethra.

questions.questionText: Pseudostratified ciliated columnar epithelium lines the:
questions.explanation: Respiratory epithelium lines most of the conducting airways.

questions.questionText: Which of the following cells produce myelin in the central nervous system?
questions.explanation: Oligodendrocytes myelinate CNS axons; Schwann cells myelinate PNS axons.

questions.questionText: The functional unit of compact bone is the:
questions.explanation: The osteon consists of concentric lamellae around a central canal.

questions.questionText: Features of cardiac muscle include all EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Cardiac muscle cells typically have a single central nucleus.

questions.questionText: Nissl bodies in neurons represent:
questions.explanation: Nissl substance is granular RER involved in protein synthesis.

questions.questionText: The principal cell type responsible for producing the extracellular matrix of cartilage is the:
questions.explanation: Chondrocytes secrete and maintain the cartilage matrix.

questions.questionText: Stratified squamous non-keratinized epithelium is found in the:
questions.explanation: Moist surfaces subject to friction like the esophagus and vagina have this.

questions.questionText: Which formed element of blood lacks a nucleus?
questions.explanation: RBCs lose their nucleus during maturation to maximize hemoglobin content.

questions.questionText: Hassall's corpuscles are a histological feature of the:
questions.explanation: Hassall's corpuscles are found in the thymic medulla.

questions.questionText: The longest and heaviest bone in the body is the:
questions.explanation: The femur is the longest and strongest bone.

questions.questionText: Which of the following is a sesamoid bone?
questions.explanation: Patella (largest) and pisiform are sesamoid bones developing within tendons.

questions.questionText: The primary ossification center for long bones typically appears in the:
questions.explanation: Primary ossification occurs in the diaphysis during fetal life.

questions.questionText: The lateral malleolus is a feature of which bone?
questions.explanation: The distal end of the fibula forms the lateral malleolus.

questions.questionText: The odontoid process (dens) is a distinguishing feature of the:
questions.explanation: The dens projects upward from the body of the axis.

questions.questionText: Which bones form the hard palate?
questions.explanation: Palatine process of maxilla and horizontal plate of palatine bone.

questions.questionText: The acetabulum is formed by the union of:
questions.explanation: All three innominate bones contribute to the acetabulum.

questions.questionText: The coracoid process is a feature of the:
questions.explanation: The coracoid process projects anteriorly from the scapula.

questions.questionText: Fracture of the surgical neck of the humerus endangers the:
questions.explanation: Axillary nerve and posterior circumflex humeral artery lie here.

questions.questionText: The pterion is an important landmark on the skull. It overlies the:
questions.optionB: Anterior branch of middle meningeal artery
questions.explanation: The pterion overlies the anterior division of the MMA.

questions.questionText: Down syndrome is most commonly caused by:
questions.explanation: Trisomy 21 due to meiotic nondisjunction is the most common cause.

questions.questionText: Klinefelter syndrome has the karyotype:
questions.explanation: 47, XXY is the classic karyotype for Klinefelter syndrome.

questions.questionText: In DNA, adenine pairs with thymine via:
questions.explanation: A-T base pairing involves 2 hydrogen bonds; G-C involves 3.

questions.questionText: The process of synthesizing RNA from a DNA template is called:
questions.explanation: Transcription is RNA synthesis catalyzed by RNA polymerase.

questions.questionText: A missense mutation results in:
questions.explanation: Missense mutations substitute one amino acid for another.

questions.questionText: Which phase of the cell cycle involves DNA replication?
questions.explanation: DNA synthesis occurs during the S (synthetic) phase.

questions.questionText: Turner syndrome usually presents with which karyotype?
questions.explanation: Monosomy X is the hallmark of Turner syndrome.

questions.questionText: The anticodon is found on which molecule?
questions.explanation: tRNA has an anticodon that base pairs with the mRNA codon.

questions.questionText: Huntington's disease exhibits a genetic phenomenon where the age of onset decreases in successive generations. This is called:
questions.explanation: Trinucleotide repeat expansion causes anticipation.

questions.questionText: Prader-Willi and Angelman syndromes are classic examples of:
questions.explanation: Imprinting involves differential expression depending on the parent of origin.
